_meta.description: REACHABLE testbed expected findings baseline. All sections include ground-truth reachability verified from source code.
_meta.no_lockfile_canary_design: hono-app and echo-app intentionally have NO package-lock.json / go.sum respectively. The scanner detects packages from package.json / go.mod with exact-pinned versions and runs Grype against a synthesized SBOM. Because no dependency graph can be built without a lockfile, all CVE findings are reported with UNKNOWN reachability. This is correct scanner behavior — the testbed entries for these apps have expected_reachability=UNKNOWN and are tested as normal assertions, not skipped.

cve.note: lodash prototype pollution — _.merge() called from /api/merge entrypoint (updated from CVE-2021-23337) (refreshed: CVE-2025-13465 → GHSA-xxjr-mmjv-4gpg)
Log4Shell: user input flows to logger.info() in live @PostMapping. Call path: HTTP → LogController.logMessage() → log4j-core (refreshed: CVE-2021-44228 → GHSA-7rjr-3q55-vv33)

JAVA CALLGRAPH CANARY: Text4Shell in DeadCodeService which has no @Service annotation and is never instantiated. NOT_REACHABLE proves Java call graph ran. UNKNOWN means it did not. (refreshed: CVE-2022-42889 → GHSA-599f-7c49-w659)
pypdf DoS — called from /api/parse-pdf (updated from CVE-2022-42969, Grype DB shift) (refreshed: CVE-2023-36464 → GHSA-2q4j-m29v-hq73)
requests CVE — module imported and called from app.py (refreshed: CVE-2023-32681 → GHSA-9hjg-9r4m-mvj7)
yaml.load in dead code — must be NOT_REACHABLE (refreshed: CVE-2020-14343 → GHSA-8q59-q68h-6hv4)
Depth-1 transitive: requests -> urllib3, called via fetch_url_reachable() (refreshed: CVE-2021-33503 → GHSA-2xpw-w6gg-jr37)
Depth-2 transitive: boto3 -> botocore -> urllib3, called via upload_to_s3_reachable() (refreshed: CVE-2021-33503 → GHSA-2xpw-w6gg-jr37)

cve_groups.note: All 3 Pillow CVEs must be grouped and all REACHABLE (CVE-2022-45199→CVE-2024-28219, Grype DB shift)
Mixed reachability in same package CVE group (CVE-2023-23931→CVE-2023-50782, Grype DB shift)
paramiko CVE in dead code — must be NOT_REACHABLE (CVE-2022-24302 removed, not in Grype DB)

cwe.note: SQL injection via string concatenation in Flask route
SQL injection in app code — must be detected even when site-packages dirs are present
TP-7: request.args → helper function → execute (cross-function taint)
EDGE-1: mixed safe+unsafe — page_size is constant but search is request.args
EDGE-2: conditional — one branch safe, one unsafe
EDGE-3: variable starts safe, overwritten with request.args
EDGE-4: user-controlled comma-separated list in IN clause
Path traversal via unsanitized file path

secrets.note: AWS key in module imported by app.py via get_api_key()
RSA private key in dead code — module never imported by app.py (was aws_access_key but file has no AWS key)
Live Stripe sk_live key used in /api/payment/charge Flask route
Twilio SID+auth used in /api/sms/send Flask route

dlp.note: SSN in plain assignment — DLP scanner treats PII presence as reachable by design
Credit card numbers — DLP scanner treats PII presence as reachable by design
SSN taint: request → LLM API — Flask route /api/summarize-patient
Credit card taint: request → anthropic API — Flask route /api/analyze-user
SSN taint: request → logger.info() — Flask route /api/register

ai.note: Standalone functions — no Flask routes, not imported by any entrypoint. AI patterns detected but code is not reachable.
Standalone functions — no Flask routes, not imported by any entrypoint
Standalone functions — no Flask routes, not imported by any entrypoint
Standalone functions — no Flask routes, not imported by any entrypoint
Classes and functions — no Flask routes, not imported by any entrypoint. Contains VulnerableToolExecutor, vulnerable_code_executor, etc.
eval(LLM output) in vulnerable_code_executor — standalone, not imported
Standalone functions — no Flask routes, not imported by any entrypoint
RAG poisoning in VulnerableRAGAgent — standalone class, not imported

malware.note: setup.py install hook — runs on pip install, reachable by definition
setup.py install hook with data exfiltration — runs on pip install
Environment variable harvesting in setup.py — runs on pip install
Python reverse shell in setup.py — runs on pip install
Dependency confusion attack — setup.py install hook
Typosquat of requests package — setup.py install hook

Transitive RAT dropper — mirrors plain-crypto-js@4.2.1 (axios supply chain attack, March 2026). postinstall fires node setup.js which makes raw TCP (net module) + HTTPS + DNS exfil attempts to sfrclak.com. Detected by Node.js network intercept preload (intercept-net.js). All deps pre-loaded in /pkgs/ so any network call = malicious. Expected: SANDBOX_EVENT network_blocked via net.Socket.connect + https.request + dns.resolve.

Two-package supply chain attack — mirrors axios@1.14.1 (March 2026 compromise). axios-dropper-simulation itself has NO postinstall (clean host pkg). Attack is in transitive dep plain-crypto-js-simulation which has postinstall RAT. Detected via full dep-tree pre-fetch: npm pack --package-lock-only resolves tree, all tarballs COPYd into sandbox image, npm install triggers transitive postinstall with network_disabled=True + intercept-net.js preload.

reachability_validation.description: [DJANGO FBV] CWE-89 sqli_fbv_parameterized — REACHABLE but parameterized, NOT a TP
reachability_validation.detection_note: Function is REACHABLE but uses parameterized query — engine should NOT flag as SQLi TP

reachability_validation.description: [DJANGO CBV] CWE-89 SQLiCBV.get() — must be REACHABLE via as_view() dispatch
reachability_validation.note: CBV resolution chain: urls.py → View.as_view() → dispatch() → get(). Engine must resolve as_view().

reachability_validation.description: [DJANGO DEAD] sqli_dead_code — NOT_REACHABLE (not in urls.py)
reachability_validation.detection_note: REACHABLE here means engine is not reading urls.py and is treating all view functions as entrypoints

reachability_validation.description: [DRF @api_view] CWE-89 drf_sqli_view — REACHABLE via path() registration

reachability_validation.description: [DRF @api_view DEAD] drf_dead_view — NOT_REACHABLE (not in urlpatterns)
reachability_validation.detection_note: REACHABLE here means engine treats @api_view as entrypoint without checking URL registration

reachability_validation.description: [DRF ViewSet] UserViewSet.list() — REACHABLE via router.register()
reachability_validation.note: DRF router.register() → ViewSet action method. Engine must resolve DefaultRouter registration.

reachability_validation.description: [DRF ViewSet @action] search_unsafe() — REACHABLE via router @action
reachability_validation.note: DRF @action decorator on ViewSet method — engine must recognise @action as generating a URL route

reachability_validation.description: [DRF ViewSet DEAD] DeadViewSet.list() — NOT_REACHABLE (not in router)
reachability_validation.detection_note: REACHABLE here means engine treats all ViewSet classes as registered regardless of router

reachability_validation.description: [FASTAPI] sqli_path_param (str) — REACHABLE, CWE-89 TP
reachability_validation.note: FastAPI @app.get with str path param — engine must recognise @app.get/@app.post as entrypoints

reachability_validation.description: [FASTAPI] sqli_path_param_safe (int annotation) — REACHABLE, not injectable (FP)
reachability_validation.detection_note: int annotation means FastAPI enforces type — flagging this as SQLi TP is a false positive

reachability_validation.description: [FASTAPI] sqli_body via Pydantic — REACHABLE, Pydantic fields are user-controlled
reachability_validation.note: FastAPI Pydantic body field flows to SQL — engine must recognise BaseModel fields as user-controlled

reachability_validation.description: [FASTAPI] sqli_depends via Depends() chain — REACHABLE
reachability_validation.note: FastAPI Depends() DI chain — user input flows through dependency. Engine must follow Depends().

reachability_validation.description: [FASTAPI APIRouter] router_sqli — REACHABLE via app.include_router()
reachability_validation.note: FastAPI APIRouter registered via app.include_router() — engine must resolve include_router()

reachability_validation.description: [FASTAPI DEAD] unrouted_sqli — NOT_REACHABLE (plain function, no decorator)
reachability_validation.detection_note: Plain function with no route decorator and never called — must be NOT_REACHABLE

reachability_validation.description: [NESTJS] UserController.getUser() — REACHABLE via AppModule.controllers[]
reachability_validation.note: NestJS @Controller + @Get method in registered controller. Engine must parse AppModule.controllers.

reachability_validation.description: [NESTJS DI] UserController.searchUsers() → UserService.findByName() — REACHABLE via DI
reachability_validation.note: NestJS DI chain: Controller → Service.findByName(). Engine must follow constructor injection.

reachability_validation.description: [NESTJS DEAD] AdminController.getAllUsers() — NOT_REACHABLE (not in AppModule)
reachability_validation.detection_note: REACHABLE here means engine doesn't check AppModule.controllers[] before marking as entrypoint

reachability_validation.description: [FASTIFY] sqli via request.query — REACHABLE via fastify.get()
reachability_validation.note: Fastify uses request.query not req.query — engine must recognise fastify.get() as entrypoint

reachability_validation.description: [FASTIFY options-object] cmd injection via handler: fn — REACHABLE
reachability_validation.note: Fastify options-object style: fastify.post(route, { handler: fn }) — engine must find handler property

reachability_validation.description: [FASTIFY fastify.route()] declarative route object — REACHABLE
reachability_validation.note: fastify.route({ method, url, handler }) declarative form — engine must parse handler field

reachability_validation.description: [FASTIFY plugin] sqli inside fastify.register() plugin — REACHABLE
reachability_validation.note: Routes inside fastify.register(plugin) are REACHABLE. Engine must follow register() to find them.
reachability_validation.detection_note: NOT_REACHABLE here means engine does not follow fastify.register() plugin system

reachability_validation.description: [FASTIFY dead plugin] deadPlugin never registered — NOT_REACHABLE
reachability_validation.detection_note: REACHABLE here means engine treats all Fastify plugin functions as registered

reachability_validation.description: [ECHO] sqliQueryParam via c.QueryParam() — REACHABLE via e.GET()
reachability_validation.note: Echo uses c.QueryParam() not c.Query() — engine must recognise e.GET() as entrypoint

reachability_validation.description: [ECHO group] sqliInGroup via e.Group().GET() — REACHABLE
reachability_validation.note: Echo e.Group() routes — engine must recognise group.GET() as equivalent to e.GET()

reachability_validation.description: [ECHO dead] deadSQLi — NOT_REACHABLE (plain function, never registered)
reachability_validation.detection_note: REACHABLE here means engine treats all func(string) functions as entrypoints

reachability_validation.description: [REACT XSS] XSSFromURLParam dangerouslySetInnerHTML with URL param — REACHABLE
reachability_validation.note: React client-side XSS: URL param → dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Reachability = component is mounted in App.

reachability_validation.description: [REACT XSS DEAD] XSSDeadComponent — NOT_REACHABLE (exported but never used in App)
reachability_validation.detection_note: REACHABLE here means engine treats all exported React components as mounted

sqli_variable_origin.description: FP-1: constant value — should NOT be flagged or should be downgraded
sqli_variable_origin.note: Semgrep sees f-string SQL but variable is a hardcoded int

sqli_variable_origin.description: FP-2: config dict value — should NOT be flagged or should be downgraded
sqli_variable_origin.note: Variable from app-level config, not user input

sqli_variable_origin.description: FP-3: env variable — should NOT be flagged or should be downgraded
sqli_variable_origin.note: Server-controlled env var, not user input
int() cast sanitizes — ValueError on non-numeric input, no injection possible

sqli_variable_origin.description: FP-5: computed value from DB result — not user input
sqli_variable_origin.note: Variable is a computed integer from internal DB query

sqli_variable_origin.description: FP-6: allowlist-validated input — safe after validation
sqli_variable_origin.note: User input constrained to known-safe values via allowlist check
Loop counter is always an int from range()

sqli_variable_origin.description: FP-8: internal function return — not user input
sqli_variable_origin.note: Variable from internal function that returns hardcoded string

sqli_variable_origin.description: TN-1: parameterized ? — should NOT be flagged
sqli_variable_origin.note: Parameterized query with ? placeholder — safe by construction

sqli_variable_origin.description: TN-2: parameterized :name — should NOT be flagged
TN-3: parameterized %s tuple — should NOT be flagged
sqli_variable_origin.note: DB-API parameterized query — safe by construction

sqli_variable_origin.description: EDGE-1: mixed safe+unsafe in same query — MUST flag
sqli_variable_origin.note: page_size is constant (safe) but search is request.args (unsafe) — must flag

sqli_variable_origin.description: EDGE-2: conditional — one branch safe, one unsafe — MUST flag
sqli_variable_origin.note: At least one code path has user-controlled input flowing to SQL

sqli_variable_origin.description: EDGE-3: reassignment — starts safe, overwritten with user input — MUST flag
sqli_variable_origin.note: Variable is reassigned from constant to request.args on one path

sqli_variable_origin.description: EDGE-4: string join with user input — MUST flag
sqli_variable_origin.note: User-controlled comma-separated list injected into IN clause
